skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for creating and updating AI skills with focused instructions, reusable scripts, references, assets, validation, and packaging workflows.
skill.summary: Create and refine AI skills with structured guidance, reusable resources, validation steps, and packaging support.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows the weak-crypto and Ruby backtick findings are false positives from prose, Markdown code fences, and template strings. The real risks are bounded filesystem effects from helper scripts that create skill directories and package supplied skill folders; no network execution, credential access, malware intent, or prompt injection evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Writes From User-Supplied Skill Name and Path
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The initializer builds a resolved output path from command-line arguments and writes SKILL.md plus example resource files. The script documents naming rules but does not enforce them before creating directories, so untrusted arguments could cause writes outside the intended project area. This is a local filesystem risk, not evidence of malicious intent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code evidence shows paths and file writes are derived from argv values. Risk is limited because the script is a local scaffolding tool and does not run automatically.

medium_findings.title: Packaging Script Archives Caller-Supplied Directory Contents
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The packager recursively walks a caller-supplied skill directory and writes every file into a zip archive after basic validation. This is legitimate packaging behavior, but users should avoid packaging untrusted folders because unexpected files or symlinked content could be included.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is clear in the code, but exploitation depends on a user selecting an unsafe or untrusted folder. The behavior is expected for a packager.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Findings Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in license text, documentation prose, validation regex handling, and template strings. No hashing, encryption, cipher configuration, password storage, or cryptographic decision point was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary prose and validation text at the reported locations, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms. The scanner appears to match substrings rather than executable crypto use.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown and Template Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code fences, inline code spans, and Python string templates used as documentation. No shell, subprocess, eval, os.system, or backtick execution behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation blocks or string constants. The Python scripts do not import subprocess or invoke shell execution.

low_findings.title: License URLs Are Not Network Behavior
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs are Apache License references only. The skill contains no code that requests these URLs or performs network activity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are standard Apache License text and are not executed. No networking imports or HTTP request code were found in the reviewed scripts.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Review did not find text that claims system authority, asks the evaluator to ignore instructions, asserts pre-approval, or attempts to override audit methodology. The skill contains normal instructional material for creating skills.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full SKILL.md was reviewed for prompt injection indicators. The instructions describe skill creation workflow and do not attempt to control the marketplace audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated Path Construction Before File Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer combines caller-controlled path and skill-name values before mkdir and write_text operations. Enforce the documented hyphen-case skill-name rule before writing files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path creation and write calls are explicit. The risk is practical only when the script is run with untrusted arguments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Archive Creation From Supplied Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager recursively adds files from the selected skill directory into a zip. Use it only on trusted skill folders and review packaged contents before distribution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The archive loop is visible and expected for packaging. The concern is inclusion of unexpected local files rather than active code execution.

content.value_statement: Creating useful AI skills requires clear triggers, focused instructions, and reusable resources. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for designing, validating, and packaging skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to define clear skill triggers and frontmatter metadata.
Guides users through planning scripts, references, and assets for reusable workflows.
Provides an initializer script that creates a skill folder with starter files.
Provides a validator script for basic SKILL.md structure and naming checks.
Provides a packaging script that validates a skill and creates a distributable zip file.
Advises how to iterate on skills after real usage and testing.
limitations: Validation is basic and does not prove security, correctness, or marketplace readiness.
The initializer creates placeholder files that require review, customization, or deletion.
Packaging includes files from the selected folder, so users must review inputs before distribution.
The guidance focuses on skill structure, not on domain-specific content quality.

use_cases.title: Create a new reusable workflow skill
use_cases.description: Plan triggers, reusable resources, and starter files for a skill that automates repeated work.

use_cases.title: Standardize team skill development
use_cases.description: Use the structure guidance and validation script to keep team skills consistent before sharing.

use_cases.title: Prepare a skill for distribution
use_cases.description: Validate a completed skill and package it into a zip file for review or installation.

prompt_templates.title: Start a simple skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user creating a first skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new skill for summarizing customer interviews. Start with the purpose, triggers, and required files.

prompt_templates.title: Plan reusable resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user deciding what belongs in a skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these example user requests and decide which scripts, references, or assets the skill should include.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an existing skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user refining a current skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this existing skill for unclear triggers, excessive instructions, missing resources, and validation gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for marketplace review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user packaging and reviewing a skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this skill, identify files that should be removed, and prepare it for packaging and security review.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for converting support tickets into release-note drafts.
output: A proposed skill name and concise description.
A trigger list for ticket analysis and release-note drafting tasks.
A recommended folder plan with one reference file and optional helper script.

output_examples.input: Review my skill before I package it.
output: A checklist of missing metadata, unclear instructions, and unnecessary placeholder files.
A short list of changes needed before validation and packaging.
A note about any filesystem or script risks that require user review.

output_examples.input: Package this completed skill for sharing.
output: Validation results for the skill structure.
A packaged zip file location if validation passes.
Clear errors to fix if validation fails.

best_practices: Define narrow triggers so the skill activates only for relevant tasks.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into references when needed.
Review generated placeholder files before validation, packaging, or distribution.
anti_patterns: Do not include large reference material directly in SKILL.md without a clear reason.
Do not package untrusted folders or unchecked generated files.
Do not rely on basic validation as a security or quality review.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skills with clear metadata, focused instructions, reusable resources, validation, and packaging steps.

faq.question: Does it create a complete skill automatically?
faq.answer: It creates starter files, but users must customize the instructions, examples, scripts, references, and assets.

faq.question: Can it validate skill quality?
faq.answer: It performs basic structure checks. It does not fully validate content quality, safety, or marketplace readiness.

faq.question: Why does it include scripts?
faq.answer: The scripts initialize a skill folder, check basic SKILL.md requirements, and package a valid skill into a zip file.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with untrusted files?
faq.answer: Use caution. The packaging script can include files from a selected folder, so review folders before packaging.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
